flowTitle: XIIGen Community Platform — Member Registration, Email Verification, and Onboarding

freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Per-tenant configurable values. Super-tenant can change once at fork time; sub-tenant can change per install. MACHINE logic around them does not change.

keys.semantic: Minimum wait between verification-email resend attempts for the same member within the same tenant. Rule (CF-3) is MACHINE; window is FREEDOM.
Who appears as the inviter on the community-invitation email. 'That an invitation exists' is MACHINE; the name is FREEDOM.
The community's display name in the invitation email body. Tenants renaming their community change this without touching MACHINE logic.
Time after which a verification token expires. Security engineering decides per tenant abuse profile. 24h default is industry-standard.

boundedRange.rationale: 5 minutes minimum (attacker clock skew); 7 days maximum (inbox delivery delays)

keys.semantic: Number of failed registration attempts from one IP in a rolling hour before CAPTCHA is required. Lower = more aggressive abuse prevention; higher = less friction for legitimate retries.
Tenant-level cap on successful registrations per hour. Protects against credential-stuffing and bulk-abuse patterns.
Days after registration before a reminder email is sent to verified-but-not-onboarded users. Product decides based on re-engagement funnel data.

grammarAdaptation.description: DSL operators or grammar elements that expand the flow's expressiveness. Tenants can add new operators without modifying MACHINE logic.
grammarAdaptation.notApplicableReason: FLOW-01 has no DSL grammar surface. Form-condition-evaluator grammar belongs to FLOW-21/23. FLOW-01 is transaction-mechanics-only.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Role-visibility matrices for client-side access control. ViewerRole + RoleScopedView define per-role UI rendering.
roleScopeAdaptation.notApplicableReason: FLOW-01 client pages (6 pages per A4 digest) use ViewerRole for rendering, but no explicit role-scope adaptation keys exist. FLOW-01 flows through standard role-aware presentation.
roleScopeAdaptation.plannedExtraction: GAP-11 Guard 4 — ViewerRole extraction to @xiigen/plugin-sdk (pending Tier 2 code work)

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Business-domain-specific edge cases a tenant can override. For FLOW-01: SSO bypass, invite-code handling, duplicate-email response shape.

edgeCases.name: SSO bypass of verification + onboarding
edgeCases.semantic: Tenants using SSO (per MT-3) can bypass email verification + onboarding delivery by setting existingOnboardingMaterialsCount=3 at completion-gate invocation.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Parameter-driven: caller passes existingOnboardingMaterialsCount=3 to skip delivery + gate.

edgeCases.name: Invite-code gated registration
edgeCases.semantic: Tenants restricting registration to invited members can use inviteCode input on RegistrationService.register to gate entry.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Optional field stored on user record; validation logic is MACHINE (ship-time) but can be overridden in forked deployments.

edgeCases.name: Uniform VALIDATION_FAILURE shape
edgeCases.semantic: Tenants with stricter compliance requirements may choose to reveal which field failed (security-vs-UX tradeoff). Current implementation is uniform: 'Registration input validation failed' regardless of field.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: MACHINE: uniform error shape per FLOW-01-RAG-03 (no field leakage in error messages).

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed architectural invariants. Tenants cannot change these without breaking cascade safety, DNA compliance, or arbiter contracts.
rules: CF-1: Email uniqueness MUST be checked BEFORE user record created (within each tenant's scope)
DNA-7: Idempotency key returns same result, no duplicate record
DNA-8: storeDocument() completes BEFORE enqueue() (outbox pattern)
DNA-9: All inter-service events use CloudEvents envelope
EV-5: Token marked 'used' on every submission — even if expired
EV-6: Supersede previous active tokens BEFORE issuing new one
FLOW-01-RAG-02: Token stored as sha256 hash — raw token only in VerificationEmailRequested event
FLOW-01-RAG-03: Uniform VALIDATION_FAILURE — no field names in error (no field leakage)
OD-6: Completion gate requires all 3 materialTypes PRESENT — failed records count, absent records do not
AF-9 score threshold for PASS verdict is 0.85 — lowering this would allow DNA violations to pass

freedom-config-example-shortened-verific.description: Tenant with high abuse profile reduces verification TTL from 24h to 1h
freedom-config-example-shortened-verific.impact: Users must click verification link within 1 hour; reduces stolen-token window.
freedom-config-example-shortened-verific.testable: Layer 1 TI-4 proves tenant-scoped FREEDOM reads; this freedom-config adaptation is addressable by setting the tenant's key value, no code change.

freedom-config-example-rebranded-invitat.description: Tenant rebrands community invitation (different organization name)
freedom-config-example-rebranded-invitat.impact: All invitation emails use Acme branding; no MACHINE code changes.
freedom-config-example-rebranded-invitat.testable: T49.deliverInvitation reads these keys at runtime; existing tests prove FREEDOM lookup works.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements of FLOW-01 that are NOT tenant-adaptable. Changing these breaks MACHINE contracts and is forbidden by arbiter gates.
elements: Rate-limit enforcement rule (only the window is FREEDOM, the rule is MACHINE)

layer_2_playwright.blockedOn: FLOW-01 has 6 client pages; Playwright harness for client pages requires GAP-24 Layer 2 infrastructure per Fix Plan v4.9

layer_3_visual.blockedOn: SK-549 visual baseline requires GAP-24 Layer 3 + GAP-27 visual regression matrix (Sprint B per Fix Plan v4.9)
